summary: Round 20 card is Broncos, Sharks, Roosters, Raiders, Warriors, Bulldogs, Titans and Dolphins. Exact market prices were not recorded, so market reads are being used as leader-behaviour estimates. Prince_of_Penrith is chasing a 5-point gap to 2nd before finals.

sources.label: NRL Late Mail: Round 20 - Broncos' boost; Dearden primed
2026 NRL season results: Round 20 fixture table

scoreboard.note: Controlled split: market likely treats Panthers as leader-safe, but Brisbane get Walsh, Mam, Reynolds, Haas and Carrigan back. Panthers also regain Cleary, Martin and Yeo, so this is high-risk and high-upside.
Favourite match: Nikora is back, Addin Fonua-Blake starts and Hynes has a strong Knights record. Newcastle regain Best but carry injury reshuffle.
Favourite match: Storm are without Munster and Hughes. Roosters miss Tedesco and Collins but regain Sam Walker and Reece Robson.
Favourite match: Raiders at GIO with Strange and Hudson Young back. Souths regain Cody Walker and Alex Johnston but lose Dayne Jennings and Jayden Sullivan.
Protect pick: Warriors regain Barnett, Capewell and Dallin Watene-Zelezniak at Go Media. Dragons still have Tyrell Sloan hamstring watch.
Protect pick but fragile: Bulldogs are at home with Preston back, while Crichton and Kikau sit in the reserves. Tigers have Api Koroisau as an outside-chance reserve and Bunty Afoa suspended.
Controlled split: Titans nearly got Storm and are at Cbus. Manly lose Ben Trbojevic to HIA protocols and Corey Waddell to an ankle injury.
Coin-flip lean: Dolphins regain Tabuai-Fidow, Bostock and Cobbo, and have won their last five at Suncorp. Cowboys have Dearden in the reserves and likely to return, so recheck final mail.

liveAlerts.text: Round 20 card live: Broncos, Sharks, Roosters, Raiders, Warriors, Bulldogs, Titans and Dolphins.
Market-aware strategy is active, but exact market prices were not recorded in this update. Treat bands as leader-behaviour estimates until final odds are checked.
Controlled splits: Broncos over Panthers and Titans over Sea Eagles. Dolphins over Cowboys is a low-confidence coin-flip lean.
Prince_of_Penrith target remains 3rd on 194, 5 points behind 2nd from the supplied ladder screenshot.
No Round 20 charity bet is locked; tipping-comp strategy does not move the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger.
